terms.definition: Measurement of what fraction of source code is executed by the test suite; common kinds are line, branch, function and statement coverage.
Percentage of source code lines executed by tests; the easiest metric, also the most easily misleading on branched code.
Percentage of conditional branches (both the `if` and `else` side) taken by tests; stricter than line coverage.
Coverage computed against only the lines changed in a PR; lets you enforce coverage on new code without punishing legacy gaps.
CI rule that fails the build if coverage falls below a threshold (or a diff-coverage threshold on new code).
The standard Python coverage measurement library; instruments bytecode and produces text/XML/HTML reports.
pytest plugin that integrates coverage.py into the pytest run (`pytest --cov=foo`).
Technique that flips one line at a time and reruns tests; a 'surviving' mutation reveals a weak assert that coverage couldn't see (`mutmut`, `cosmic-ray`).
Directive (`# pragma: no cover`, `omit`) that tells the coverage tool to ignore certain lines or files; should always be accompanied by a reason.
